pi_items.source: Parsed hr_schema.sql and confirmed exactly 107 employee inserts, satisfying the >= 100 threshold defined in verifier.py.
Calculated department averages from hr_schema.sql and found exactly 9 employees whose salary is >150% or <50% of their department's average, satisfying the >= 1 threshold in verifier.py.
Inspected verifier.py which explicitly requires and checks for the existence of these two tables.
Inspected verifier.py which explicitly requires these procedures to have VALID status.
Inspected verifier.py which requires the existence of these DBMS_SCHEDULER jobs.
Inspected setup_oracle.sh which creates the 'hr' user to hold the HR sample schema.
Inspected setup_oracle.sh which sets the password for the 'hr' user to 'hr123'.

privileged_info_summary: The Oracle Database is loaded with the standard HR sample schema containing exactly 107 synthetic employee records. Analysis of the data reveals exactly 9 salary anomalies (where salary is >150% or <50% of the department average). The task requires creating two tables (COMPENSATION_SNAPSHOTS, SALARY_ANOMALIES), two stored procedures (CAPTURE_COMP_SNAPSHOT, DETECT_SALARY_ANOMALIES), and two scheduled jobs (MONTHLY_COMP_SNAPSHOT, DAILY_ANOMALY_CHECK) using the HR user (password hr123). All claimed values in the task metadata are accurate constraints according to the verifier logic.
